hardware: NVIDIA H100 PCIe 80GB 1x (Scaleway via NVIDIA Brev — instance apohara-h100-bench2)
honesty_note: Real H100 measurement on 1x NVIDIA H100 PCIe 80GB via Scaleway/NVIDIA Brev (instance apohara-h100-bench2). Model: Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B (FP16, dense, ~54 GB weights). HuggingFace transformers 5.8.1 backend; vLLM 0.21.0 does not yet recognize the qwen3_5 model_type introduced by Qwen3.6, so the production vLLM-plugin path is deferred until vLLM upstream lands qwen3_5 support. Both runs executed the same 5-agent pipeline (retriever -> reranker -> summarizer -> critic -> responder) with the same seed=0. Baseline: each agent encodes the full ~2.3K-token shared context + its agent-specific suffix from scratch. Contextforge: each non-critic agent encodes ONLY its agent-specific suffix (the shared prefix is assumed already in the KV registry, the compression ContextForge productizes). The critic agent (use_dense=True, INV-15 gate fired with risk 0.90 > threshold 0.65) re-encodes the full prompt — this is the safety override the paper specifies. The 0.22% peak-VRAM delta is the SEQUENTIAL single-agent peak measurement; the architectural 76% saving in the paper is for CONCURRENT multi-agent KV-sharing (5 agents holding KV concurrently), which requires the vLLM plugin path. What this log proves: (1) the system runs end-to-end on real H100 with a 2026-era Qwen3.6 model, (2) INV-15 gate fires correctly on the critic agent (1/5 fires, critic_dense=1) under both modes, (3) Qwen3.6-27B's hybrid linear-attention architecture imposes practical constraints on transformers' past_key_values reuse path that the production vLLM plugin addresses via its own KV registry.
